title: Creation to the Patriarchs
unitGoal: Students practise the first major Bible timeline section from creation through Joseph with a creation-focused foundation.

lessons.title: God Created Everything
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise the Bible's teaching that God created everything by His Word and that creation belongs to Him.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise the Bible's teaching that God created everything by His Word and that creation belongs to Him.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: God Created Everything Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand creation as God's true work, not an accident or an unguided process.

steps.title: The Beginning
steps.prompt: Which statement best matches Genesis 1:1?
choices: God created the heavens and the earth in the beginning
God gave order to matter that was already eternal
God began working after creation already existed in a rough form
steps.correctAnswer: God created the heavens and the earth in the beginning
steps.feedback: Correct. Genesis begins with God creating the heavens and the earth.
steps.hint: The Bible starts with God, not with matter or chance.

steps.title: By God's Word
steps.prompt: Genesis 1 shows God creating mainly by...
choices: speaking His powerful word
forming the world through a process He did not direct by His word
waiting for nature to arrange itself without His command
steps.correctAnswer: speaking His powerful word
steps.feedback: Yes. God speaks, and creation obeys.
steps.hint: Look for the repeated words, 'And God said.'

steps.title: Six Days
steps.prompt: Which answer best fits the Bible's creation week?
choices: God created in six days and rested on the seventh
God created in a way that cannot be connected to days
God began creation and then waited for long ages to finish it
steps.correctAnswer: God created in six days and rested on the seventh
steps.feedback: Correct. Scripture presents creation in six days, with God resting on the seventh.
steps.hint: Exodus 20:11 also connects creation with six days and the Sabbath.

steps.title: Very Good
steps.prompt: When God finished creating, He called His creation...
choices: very good
unfinished but improving
mostly good except for the physical world
steps.correctAnswer: very good
steps.feedback: Right. God's finished creation was very good.
steps.hint: The problem of sin comes after creation, not as part of God's good design.

steps.title: No Accident
steps.prompt: Which sentence best fits a Christian view of creation?
choices: Creation exists because God made it on purpose
Creation slowly became meaningful when people noticed it
Creation can be explained without God's command or design
steps.correctAnswer: Creation exists because God made it on purpose
steps.feedback: Yes. Creation has purpose because God created it.
steps.hint: The Bible teaches that God is Creator and Lord.

steps.title: True or False
steps.prompt: The Bible teaches that God made the world and all life.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. God created the world and all life.
steps.hint: Creation belongs to God because He made it.

steps.title: Creator's Authority
steps.prompt: Because God created everything, He has the right to...
choices: rule creation and tell people what is good
let creation decide what is good
rule only the spiritual parts of life
steps.correctAnswer: rule creation and tell people what is good
steps.feedback: Correct. The Creator has authority over what He made.
steps.hint: God's authority comes before human opinion.

steps.title: A Good Response
steps.prompt: A right response to God as Creator is...
choices: worship, gratitude, and obedience
respect for nature without worshiping God
valuing creation while forgetting it belongs to God
steps.correctAnswer: worship, gratitude, and obedience
steps.feedback: Yes. Creation should lead us to worship and obey the Creator.
steps.hint: We care for creation because it belongs to God.

steps.title: Creation and Science
steps.prompt: When Christians study creation, they should remember that...
choices: God's Word is true and creation is His world
science replaces the need for God's Word
the Bible matters for worship but not for origins
steps.correctAnswer: God's Word is true and creation is His world
steps.feedback: Right. God's Word is true, and creation is His world.
steps.hint: Careful study should not be separated from trust in God's Word.

questions.prompt: Genesis 1:1 teaches that...
choices: God created the heavens and the earth
God found a world that was already complete
God gave purpose to matter He did not create
questions.correctAnswer: God created the heavens and the earth
questions.reviewNote: Genesis begins with God as Creator.

questions.prompt: Which answer best fits the Bible's creation week?
choices: God created in six days and rested on the seventh
God created slowly through long ages before resting
God rested first and then creation developed
questions.correctAnswer: God created in six days and rested on the seventh
questions.reviewNote: Scripture presents creation in six days and rest on the seventh.

questions.prompt: God's finished creation was very good before sin entered the world.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Sin enters after God's very good creation.

questions.prompt: Creation has purpose because...
choices: God made it on purpose
people later decided it was meaningful
nature gave itself meaning over time
questions.reviewNote: Creation has purpose because God created it.

questions.prompt: Because God is Creator, people should respond with...
choices: worship, gratitude, and obedience
self-rule apart from God
interest in nature without trust in God
questions.reviewNote: God as Creator deserves worship and obedience.

lessons.title: Made in God's Image
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise the Bible's teaching that every human being has dignity because people are made in God's image.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise the Bible's teaching that every human being has dignity because people are made in God's image.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Made in God's Image Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand human dignity, male and female, sacred life, stewardship, and marriage as God's good design.

steps.title: Image of God
steps.prompt: Being made in God's image means human beings...
choices: reflect God in a special way among His creatures
represent God while no longer needing His authority
receive worth mainly through ability and wise choices
steps.correctAnswer: reflect God in a special way among His creatures
steps.feedback: Correct. People reflect God in a special way among His creatures.
steps.hint: Image of God is about God's design and gift, not earned status.

steps.title: Where Worth Comes From
steps.prompt: A person's worth comes first from...
choices: being made by God in His image
being useful to other people
being old enough to make choices
steps.correctAnswer: being made by God in His image
steps.feedback: Yes. Human worth is given by God.
steps.hint: Worth does not depend on age, ability, health, or popularity.

steps.title: True or False
steps.prompt: Every human life is sacred because people are made by God in His image.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. Human life is sacred because people bear God's image.
steps.hint: This includes the smallest, weakest, youngest, and oldest people.

steps.title: God's Design
steps.prompt: Genesis teaches that God created humanity...
choices: male and female in His image
with bodies that do not matter to identity
with different value depending on their roles
steps.correctAnswer: male and female in His image
steps.feedback: Right. Male and female are both made in God's image.
steps.hint: Both men and women have equal dignity before God.

steps.title: Bodies Matter
steps.prompt: Which sentence best fits the Bible's view of the human body?
choices: God made our bodies, so they are part of His good design
The body matters less than what a person feels inside
Bodies are only temporary shells with no created meaning
steps.correctAnswer: God made our bodies, so they are part of His good design
steps.feedback: Correct. God made human bodies as part of His good design.
steps.hint: Creation includes the body, not only the mind or feelings.

steps.title: Marriage
steps.prompt: Genesis 2 presents marriage as God's design for...
choices: one man and one woman
any close friendship that people choose
a promise that can mean different things in every generation
steps.correctAnswer: one man and one woman
steps.feedback: Correct. Genesis presents marriage as one man and one woman.
steps.hint: Jesus also points back to this creation design.

steps.title: Ruling Creation
steps.prompt: When God tells people to rule over creation, He means they should...
choices: care for it under His authority
use it selfishly because people are more important
worship creation as equal with the Creator
steps.correctAnswer: care for it under His authority
steps.feedback: Yes. People are stewards under God's authority.
steps.hint: Stewardship means responsible care for what belongs to God.

steps.title: Equal Dignity
steps.prompt: Which example best shows belief in the image of God?
choices: Treating people with dignity even when they are weak or different
Respecting people more when they are successful
Giving worth only to people who agree with us
steps.correctAnswer: Treating people with dignity even when they are weak or different
steps.feedback: Correct. The image of God calls us to honor every human being.
steps.hint: Dignity is not earned by success or agreement.

steps.title: Love and Truth
steps.prompt: A Christian view of human dignity should include...
choices: kindness joined with God's truth
kindness that avoids every hard truth
truth spoken without patience or mercy
steps.correctAnswer: kindness joined with God's truth
steps.feedback: Right. Christians should hold kindness and truth together.
steps.hint: Love does not erase God's design, and truth should not be harsh.

questions.prompt: Human beings have worth because...
choices: they are made by God in His image
they become valuable after helping others
they choose their own purpose first
questions.correctAnswer: they are made by God in His image
questions.reviewNote: Worth comes from God's creation and image.

questions.prompt: Every human life is sacred because people bear God's image.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Human life is sacred because God made people in His image.

questions.prompt: Genesis teaches that God created humanity...
choices: male and female in His image
with bodies that have no created meaning
with worth only inside marriage
questions.correctAnswer: male and female in His image
questions.reviewNote: Male and female are both made in God's image.

questions.prompt: Genesis 2 presents marriage as...
choices: one man and one woman
any relationship with strong feelings
a human idea later added to creation
questions.reviewNote: Genesis presents marriage as one man and one woman.

questions.prompt: A steward of creation should...
choices: care for creation under God's authority
treat creation as useful but not entrusted by God
treat creation as equal with God
questions.correctAnswer: care for creation under God's authority
questions.reviewNote: People care for creation because it belongs to God.

lessons.title: The Fall
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise the Bible's teaching about temptation, sin, judgment, mercy, and God's first promise of rescue.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise the Bible's teaching about temptation, sin, judgment, mercy, and God's first promise of rescue.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: The Fall Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand what happened when sin entered God's good world and why God's mercy still matters.

steps.title: The Serpent's Question
steps.prompt: The serpent's first question pushed Eve to...
choices: doubt God's word and goodness
ask Adam to explain the garden rules
study creation more carefully
steps.correctAnswer: doubt God's word and goodness
steps.feedback: Correct. The serpent attacked trust in God's word and goodness.
steps.hint: Temptation often begins by twisting what God has said.

steps.title: What Changed
steps.prompt: What did Adam and Eve do in Genesis 3?
choices: They disobeyed God's command
They misunderstood a command God had not clearly given
They discovered that God's warning was only symbolic
steps.correctAnswer: They disobeyed God's command
steps.feedback: Yes. Adam and Eve chose to disobey God's command.
steps.hint: Sin is not only confusion. It is rebellion against God.

steps.title: True or False
steps.prompt: The Fall happened after God's creation had already been declared very good.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. Sin entered after God's very good creation.
steps.hint: The Bible does not make sin part of God's good design.

steps.title: Hiding
steps.prompt: After Adam and Eve sinned, they...
choices: hid from God in shame and fear
came to God honestly without fear
lost interest in blaming anyone
steps.correctAnswer: hid from God in shame and fear
steps.feedback: Right. Sin brought shame, fear, and hiding.
steps.hint: Their relationship with God was broken.

steps.title: Blame
steps.prompt: When God questioned Adam and Eve, they showed...
choices: blame shifting instead of humble confession
complete repentance without excuses
that sin had not affected their relationships
steps.correctAnswer: blame shifting instead of humble confession
steps.feedback: Correct. They shifted blame rather than confessing humbly.
steps.hint: Sin damages relationships with God and with other people.

steps.title: Result of Sin
steps.prompt: According to the Bible, sin brought...
choices: death, brokenness, and separation from God
a better way for people to become wise
a private choice with no effect beyond Adam and Eve
steps.correctAnswer: death, brokenness, and separation from God
steps.feedback: Yes. Sin brought death and brokenness into the world.
steps.hint: Romans 5:12 connects sin with death.

steps.title: God Searched
steps.prompt: When God called, 'Where are you?' He was showing...
choices: that He came to deal with sin and seek the sinners
that He did not know where Adam and Eve were
that He was giving them a way to hide the consequences
steps.correctAnswer: that He came to deal with sin and seek the sinners
steps.feedback: Correct. God knew everything, but He came to confront and seek them.
steps.hint: God's question invited them into the truth.

steps.title: First Promise
steps.prompt: Genesis 3:15 points forward to...
choices: God's promised victory over the serpent
people repairing sin by their own effort
a time when sin would no longer need judgment
steps.correctAnswer: God's promised victory over the serpent
steps.feedback: Right. Genesis 3:15 gives the first promise of rescue.
steps.hint: Christians see this promise fulfilled in Christ.

steps.title: Mercy and Consequences
steps.prompt: Which sentence best describes Genesis 3?
choices: God judged sin and also showed mercy
God showed mercy by saying sin did not matter
God gave consequences but no promise of future rescue
steps.correctAnswer: God judged sin and also showed mercy
steps.feedback: Correct. Genesis 3 includes both judgment and mercy.
steps.hint: God's mercy does not pretend sin is harmless.

questions.prompt: The serpent tempted Eve by pushing her to...
choices: doubt God's word and goodness
ask more careful questions about obedience
remember God's command more clearly
questions.reviewNote: The serpent twisted God's word and challenged His goodness.

questions.prompt: Adam and Eve's sin was...
choices: disobeying God's command
eating from every tree in the garden
asking God for wisdom
questions.reviewNote: They disobeyed the command God gave.

questions.prompt: Sin brought death and brokenness into God's good world.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Sin brought death and brokenness.

questions.prompt: Genesis 3:15 points forward to...
choices: God's promised victory over the serpent
human effort fixing sin completely
people slowly learning to fix sin by wisdom
questions.correctAnswer: God's promised victory over the serpent
questions.reviewNote: Genesis 3:15 points to God's promise of rescue.

questions.prompt: Genesis 3 shows that God...
choices: judges sin and shows mercy
ignores sin when people are ashamed
shows mercy by removing consequences
questions.reviewNote: God's judgment and mercy both appear in Genesis 3.

lessons.title: Cain, Abel, and the Spread of Sin
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise recognizing worship, anger, murder, mercy, and the spread of sin after the Fall.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise recognizing worship, anger, murder, mercy, and the spread of sin after the Fall.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Cain, Abel, and the Spread of Sin Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand Cain and Abel as a serious account of worship, anger, sin, and mercy.

steps.title: Cain and Abel
steps.prompt: Cain and Abel both brought...
choices: offerings to the Lord
animals from the flock
grain from the ground
steps.correctAnswer: offerings to the Lord
steps.feedback: Correct. Both brothers brought offerings, but God regarded Abel and his offering.
steps.hint: The difference is deeper than simply naming the object brought.

steps.title: Abel's Faith
steps.prompt: Hebrews 11 helps us understand Abel's offering by connecting it with...
choices: faith
being the older brother
offering from better material
steps.correctAnswer: faith
steps.feedback: Yes. Hebrews connects Abel's offering with faith.
steps.hint: The Bible points us beyond the surface details.

steps.title: Cain's Response
steps.prompt: When Cain's offering was not regarded, Cain became...
choices: angry and downcast
humble and quick to repent
confused but peaceful
steps.correctAnswer: angry and downcast
steps.feedback: Correct. Cain became angry and downcast.
steps.hint: God warned Cain before Cain murdered Abel.

steps.title: Crouching Sin
steps.prompt: God warned Cain that sin was...
choices: crouching at the door and desired to rule him
already gone because Cain felt sorry
mostly a feeling that would pass if ignored
steps.correctAnswer: crouching at the door and desired to rule him
steps.feedback: Right. God warned Cain that sin wanted to rule him.
steps.hint: Cain needed to master sin, not feed it.

steps.title: Anger
steps.prompt: Cain's story shows that anger can become dangerous when...
choices: it is nursed instead of brought under God's warning
a person has a strong feeling for a short time
someone asks an honest question
steps.correctAnswer: it is nursed instead of brought under God's warning
steps.feedback: Correct. Cain let anger grow into murder.
steps.hint: The warning came before the murder.

steps.title: True or False
steps.prompt: The Bible treats Abel's murder as a serious sin against God and against a human being made in God's image.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. Murder is serious because human life belongs to God.
steps.hint: Genesis 4 shows how quickly sin spread after the Fall.

steps.title: Where Is Abel?
steps.prompt: God's question, 'Where is Abel your brother?' gave Cain...
choices: a chance to tell the truth
new information God did not have
permission to explain why Abel deserved harm
steps.correctAnswer: a chance to tell the truth
steps.feedback: Correct. God knew what happened and confronted Cain.
steps.hint: God's questions often bring sinners into the light.

steps.title: Cain's Punishment
steps.prompt: Cain's punishment included...
choices: being driven from the ground and becoming a wanderer
being made king over his family
being told that murder was not a lasting problem
steps.correctAnswer: being driven from the ground and becoming a wanderer
steps.feedback: Yes. Cain was judged and became a wanderer.
steps.hint: God's judgment fit Cain's sin and life.

steps.title: God's Mark
steps.prompt: The mark God gave Cain showed...
choices: mercy that protected Cain from being killed
a sign that Cain's punishment was finished
that Abel's life was less important
steps.correctAnswer: mercy that protected Cain from being killed
steps.feedback: Correct. God judged Cain but also showed mercy.
steps.hint: Mercy did not mean Cain's sin was excused.

quiz.title: Cain, Abel, and the Spread of Sin Quiz

questions.prompt: Cain and Abel both brought...
choices: offerings to the Lord
animals from the flock
grain from the ground
questions.reviewNote: Both brought offerings, but Abel's was connected with faith.

questions.prompt: Hebrews 11 connects Abel's offering with...
choices: faith
birth order
better farming
questions.reviewNote: Hebrews says Abel offered by faith.

questions.prompt: God warned Cain that sin was...
choices: crouching at the door
mostly just a feeling Cain could handle later
mostly a family disagreement
questions.reviewNote: God warned Cain before Cain murdered Abel.

questions.prompt: Cain's murder of Abel shows sin spreading after the Fall.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Genesis 4 shows sin spreading in human relationships.

questions.prompt: The mark God gave Cain showed...
choices: mercy and protection after judgment
a sign that Cain's punishment was finished
that Cain did not need correction
questions.reviewNote: God judged Cain and still showed mercy.

lessons.title: Noah and the Flood
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise the Bible's teaching about human wickedness, God's judgment, rescue through the ark, covenant, and faithfulness.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise the Bible's teaching about human wickedness, God's judgment, rescue through the ark, covenant, and faithfulness.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Noah and the Flood Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand the Flood as both judgment and rescue, with God's covenant faithfulness after it.

steps.title: The World in Noah's Day
steps.prompt: Genesis says the world in Noah's day was marked by...
choices: great human wickedness and violence
minor mistakes that people were already fixing
people mostly faithful but needing a stronger warning
steps.correctAnswer: great human wickedness and violence
steps.feedback: Correct. Genesis describes great wickedness and violence.
steps.hint: The Flood is not presented as a small correction.

steps.title: Noah Found Grace
steps.prompt: Noah found favor with God, which means...
choices: God showed grace to Noah
Noah lived without needing God's mercy
Noah earned rescue by being better than everyone else
steps.correctAnswer: God showed grace to Noah
steps.feedback: Yes. Noah found grace or favor with God.
steps.hint: Noah obeyed God, but rescue was still God's mercy.

steps.title: True or False
steps.prompt: Hebrews 11 describes Noah as acting by faith when he built the ark.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. Hebrews connects Noah's obedience with faith.
steps.hint: Faith listens to God's warning.

steps.title: The Ark
steps.prompt: The ark was God's appointed way to...
choices: rescue Noah, his family, and the animals He brought
help Noah escape without caring for any living creatures
prove that people could survive judgment by their own skill
steps.correctAnswer: rescue Noah, his family, and the animals He brought
steps.feedback: Correct. God used the ark as His appointed rescue.
steps.hint: The ark was not Noah's idea apart from God's command.

steps.title: The Flood
steps.prompt: The Flood shows that God's judgment is...
choices: real and serious
a lesson about consequences more than real judgment
opposed to His mercy in every way
steps.correctAnswer: real and serious
steps.feedback: Right. God's judgment is real and serious.
steps.hint: The same account also shows God's mercy in rescue.

steps.title: God Shut the Door
steps.prompt: When Genesis says the Lord shut Noah in, it shows...
choices: God's care and control over the rescue
Noah's final step of obedience after finishing the ark
the family's decision that the Flood was beginning
steps.correctAnswer: God's care and control over the rescue
steps.feedback: Correct. God was in control of the rescue and judgment.
steps.hint: The Lord, not chance, ruled the event.

steps.title: After the Flood
steps.prompt: After the Flood, Noah built...
choices: an altar to worship the Lord
a city to keep people together forever
a tower to protect people from another flood
steps.correctAnswer: an altar to worship the Lord
steps.feedback: Yes. Noah responded with worship.
steps.hint: Rescue should lead to worship and gratitude.

steps.title: The Rainbow
steps.prompt: The rainbow sign reminded Noah's family that...
choices: God would keep His covenant promise
storms would never happen again
the Flood had been only a natural disaster
steps.correctAnswer: God would keep His covenant promise
steps.feedback: Correct. The rainbow was a sign of God's covenant promise.
steps.hint: The promise was about never again destroying all flesh with a flood.

steps.title: Judgment and Mercy
steps.prompt: Which sentence best describes Noah and the Flood?
choices: God judged wickedness and rescued by grace
God ignored wickedness because Noah was kind
God rescued Noah because judgment was unnecessary
steps.correctAnswer: God judged wickedness and rescued by grace
steps.feedback: Correct. The account shows both judgment and gracious rescue.
steps.hint: Judgment and mercy both matter in the Flood account.

questions.prompt: Genesis describes Noah's world as full of...
choices: great wickedness and violence
small problems that were improving
faithfulness among most people
questions.reviewNote: Genesis describes great human wickedness and violence.

questions.prompt: Noah found favor with God means...
choices: God showed grace to Noah
Noah's obedience earned rescue without grace
Noah rescued himself
questions.reviewNote: Favor points to God's grace.

questions.prompt: The ark was God's appointed way to...
choices: rescue Noah, his family, and the animals
give Noah time to persuade others by his own plan
avoid judging wickedness
questions.correctAnswer: rescue Noah, his family, and the animals
questions.reviewNote: God commanded the ark for rescue.

questions.prompt: The rainbow was a sign of God's covenant promise.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: The rainbow was a sign of God's covenant.

questions.prompt: The Flood account shows...
choices: judgment against sin and rescue by grace
mercy without judgment
judgment without any rescue
questions.correctAnswer: judgment against sin and rescue by grace
questions.reviewNote: The account shows both judgment and mercy.

lessons.title: Babel
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise the Bible's teaching about pride, disobedience, languages, nations, and God's rule over people.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise the Bible's teaching about pride, disobedience, languages, nations, and God's rule over people.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Babel Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand Babel as a story about pride, disobedience, judgment, and God's rule over the nations.

steps.title: One Language
steps.prompt: At Babel, the people began with...
choices: one language and a shared plan
many languages and no way to work together
separate nations already spread across the earth
steps.correctAnswer: one language and a shared plan
steps.feedback: Correct. Genesis 11 begins with one language and shared words.
steps.hint: The scattering comes later.

steps.title: A Name for Themselves
steps.prompt: The people wanted to make a name for themselves, which showed...
choices: pride and self-glory
humble worship of God
obedience to fill the earth
steps.correctAnswer: pride and self-glory
steps.feedback: Right. They wanted glory for themselves.
steps.hint: Their plan was centered on their own name.

steps.title: Staying Together
steps.prompt: The people's desire not to be scattered was a problem because...
choices: God had commanded people to fill the earth
staying together mattered more than filling the earth
a strong city could keep people secure without God's command
steps.correctAnswer: God had commanded people to fill the earth
steps.feedback: Correct. The problem was proud disobedience, not bricks or building.
steps.hint: God had told people to fill the earth.

steps.title: God Came Down
steps.prompt: When Genesis says the Lord came down, it shows...
choices: human pride is small before God
people could build high enough to reach God's throne
the tower had nearly reached heaven
steps.correctAnswer: human pride is small before God
steps.feedback: Yes. The phrase shows how small human pride is before God.
steps.hint: The people thought they were great, but God is the true Lord.

steps.title: Confused Language
steps.prompt: God confused the people's language so that...
choices: their proud plan could not continue as before
they could worship Him in only one place
their unity would become stronger in smaller groups
steps.correctAnswer: their proud plan could not continue as before
steps.feedback: Correct. Their plan was stopped by God's judgment.
steps.hint: God's judgment also spread people across the earth.

steps.title: True or False
steps.prompt: Babel teaches that God rules over all nations and languages.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. God rules over peoples, nations, and languages.
steps.hint: Babel is not outside God's rule.

steps.title: Main Sin
steps.prompt: The main sin at Babel was...
choices: proud self-glory and disobedience
working together on a difficult project
using strong materials to build a city
steps.correctAnswer: proud self-glory and disobedience
steps.feedback: Right. The problem was pride and disobedience.
steps.hint: Working together is not sinful by itself.

steps.title: Scattered
steps.prompt: After Babel, people were scattered...
choices: over the face of the earth
into nearby families but not into nations yet
only after Abraham's family began
steps.correctAnswer: over the face of the earth
steps.feedback: Correct. God scattered them over the earth.
steps.hint: This moved people toward filling the earth.

steps.title: A Better Name
steps.prompt: Babel warns us that people should seek...
choices: God's glory above their own name
a famous name before obeying God
unity even when it means disobeying God
steps.correctAnswer: God's glory above their own name
steps.feedback: Yes. People should seek God's glory, not proud self-glory.
steps.hint: Unity is good only when it is joined to obedience.

questions.prompt: The people at Babel wanted to...
choices: make a name for themselves
scatter in obedience to God
build an altar after rescue
questions.reviewNote: Their plan centered on their own name.

questions.prompt: Their plan not to be scattered was wrong because...
choices: God had commanded people to fill the earth
staying together mattered more than filling the earth
a strong city could keep people secure without God's command
questions.correctAnswer: God had commanded people to fill the earth
questions.reviewNote: The problem was proud disobedience.

questions.prompt: God confused their language so that...
choices: their proud plan could not continue as before
all languages would become unimportant
they could finish the tower more safely
questions.correctAnswer: their proud plan could not continue as before
questions.reviewNote: God stopped their proud plan.

questions.prompt: Babel teaches that God rules over nations and languages.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: God rules over all peoples and languages.

questions.prompt: Babel warns people to seek...
choices: God's glory above their own name
unity even in disobedience
fame before obedience
questions.correctAnswer: God's glory above their own name
questions.reviewNote: Babel warns against proud self-glory.

lessons.title: Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise covenant promises, faith, family failure, and God's faithfulness through Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise covenant promises, faith, family failure, and God's faithfulness through Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand God's covenant promises and His faithfulness through Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

steps.title: Called by God
steps.prompt: When God called Abram, He told him to...
choices: leave his country and go where God would show him
stay in Babel and build a stronger family name
earn God's promise by first becoming a king
steps.correctAnswer: leave his country and go where God would show him
steps.feedback: Correct. Abram was called to leave and trust God's promise.
steps.hint: Faith followed God's call.

steps.title: Three Promise Themes
steps.prompt: God's promises to Abraham included...
choices: land, offspring, and blessing
a throne, a temple, and exile
a flood, a tower, and new languages
steps.correctAnswer: land, offspring, and blessing
steps.feedback: Yes. Land, offspring, and blessing are major promise themes.
steps.hint: God promised to bless all families of the earth through Abraham.

steps.title: Faith Counted
steps.prompt: Genesis 15 says Abram believed the Lord, and God...
choices: counted it to him as righteousness
waited until Abram had no doubts before blessing him
made Abram earn the promise by perfect obedience first
steps.correctAnswer: counted it to him as righteousness
steps.feedback: Correct. Abram believed God, and it was counted to him as righteousness.
steps.hint: God's promise rests on His grace and faithfulness.

steps.title: Promised Son
steps.prompt: Isaac's birth showed that...
choices: God keeps promises even when they seem impossible
Isaac's birth depended mainly on Abraham and Sarah's planning
the promise could continue through any family line
steps.correctAnswer: God keeps promises even when they seem impossible
steps.feedback: Right. Isaac's birth showed God's faithfulness.
steps.hint: Sarah was past the normal age for having children.

steps.title: Mount Moriah
steps.prompt: When God tested Abraham with Isaac, the account showed...
choices: God's provision and Abraham's trust
Abraham had to prove he deserved Isaac
that human sacrifice became God's normal command
steps.correctAnswer: God's provision and Abraham's trust
steps.feedback: Correct. God provided the ram, and Abraham trusted Him.
steps.hint: The Lord provided in the place of Isaac.

steps.title: True or False
steps.prompt: God's covenant promise continued through Isaac.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. The covenant promise continued through Isaac.
steps.hint: God kept His promise across generations.

steps.title: Jacob's Family
steps.prompt: Jacob's story shows that God's plan continued...
choices: despite family sin and conflict
because Jacob's family usually made faithful choices
because Jacob finally made up for earlier sins
steps.correctAnswer: despite family sin and conflict
steps.feedback: Yes. God's faithfulness continued despite family sin and conflict.
steps.hint: The Bible tells the truth about family failures.

steps.title: Bethel
steps.prompt: At Bethel, God promised Jacob...
choices: His presence and covenant faithfulness
freedom from every future family problem
success because Jacob had already learned every lesson
steps.correctAnswer: His presence and covenant faithfulness
steps.feedback: Correct. God repeated covenant promises and promised His presence.
steps.hint: Jacob received God's promise by grace.

steps.title: God's Faithfulness
steps.prompt: The patriarch stories teach that...
choices: God keeps His promises through imperfect people
God's promises fail when families struggle
God's promises depend on the strongest person in the family
steps.correctAnswer: God keeps His promises through imperfect people
steps.feedback: Right. God is faithful even when His people are weak.
steps.hint: The main hero of these accounts is God.

questions.prompt: God called Abram to...
choices: leave his country and go where God would show him
stay in Babel and make his name great
build an ark for his family
questions.correctAnswer: leave his country and go where God would show him
questions.reviewNote: Abram was called to leave and trust God.

questions.prompt: God's promises to Abraham included...
choices: land, offspring, and blessing
law, temple, and exile
flood, rainbow, and languages
questions.reviewNote: These are major covenant promise themes.

questions.prompt: Isaac's birth showed that God keeps promises even when they seem impossible.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Isaac's birth showed God's faithfulness.

questions.prompt: Jacob's story shows God's plan continuing...
choices: despite family sin and conflict
because Jacob's family usually made faithful choices
because Jacob finally made up for earlier sins
questions.reviewNote: God kept His promises despite family failures.

questions.prompt: The patriarch stories especially show...
choices: God's faithfulness to His covenant promises
human families fixing sin by themselves
the end of God's promise after Abraham
questions.correctAnswer: God's faithfulness to His covenant promises
questions.reviewNote: God is faithful to His promises.

lessons.title: Joseph and Providence
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise Joseph's story as an account of evil choices, suffering, forgiveness, and God's good purposes.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise Joseph's story as an account of evil choices, suffering, forgiveness, and God's good purposes.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Joseph and Providence Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand how God worked through Joseph's suffering without excusing the evil done to him.

steps.title: Family Jealousy
steps.prompt: Joseph's brothers sold him because they were...
choices: jealous and hateful toward him
trying to protect him from Egypt
obeying a clear command from God
steps.correctAnswer: jealous and hateful toward him
steps.feedback: Correct. Their jealousy and hatred led to evil choices.
steps.hint: God later used the situation, but their action was still wrong.

steps.title: In Egypt
steps.prompt: In Egypt, Joseph experienced...
choices: both suffering and God's faithful presence
hardship that God used right away to make life easy
success because no one sinned against him
steps.correctAnswer: both suffering and God's faithful presence
steps.feedback: Yes. Joseph suffered, and the Lord was with him.
steps.hint: Genesis repeats that the Lord was with Joseph.

steps.title: True or False
steps.prompt: Joseph was treated unjustly, but God had not abandoned him.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. Joseph suffered unjustly, but God was with him.
steps.hint: God's presence does not mean life is always easy.

steps.title: Dreams
steps.prompt: Joseph made clear that interpreting dreams belonged to...
choices: God
Joseph's natural cleverness
Pharaoh's court training
steps.correctAnswer: God
steps.feedback: Correct. Joseph gave credit to God.
steps.hint: Joseph did not act as if the wisdom came from himself.

steps.title: Famine
steps.prompt: God used Joseph in Egypt to...
choices: preserve many lives during famine
punish his brothers without mercy
make Egypt replace God's covenant family
steps.correctAnswer: preserve many lives during famine
steps.feedback: Right. God used Joseph to preserve many lives.
steps.hint: This included Joseph's own family.

steps.title: Responsibility
steps.prompt: Joseph's leadership in Egypt showed...
choices: wisdom, planning, and service under God's providence
revenge against everyone who had wronged him
that faithful service removes the need for careful planning
steps.correctAnswer: wisdom, planning, and service under God's providence
steps.feedback: Correct. Joseph served wisely under God's providence.
steps.hint: Providence means God's wise rule over events.

steps.title: His Brothers Return
steps.prompt: When Joseph saw his brothers again, he eventually...
choices: forgave them and cared for his family
pretended their sin had never mattered
tested them but held forgiveness back permanently
steps.correctAnswer: forgave them and cared for his family
steps.feedback: Yes. Joseph forgave and cared for them.
steps.hint: Forgiveness did not mean their sin was good.

steps.title: Genesis 50:20
steps.prompt: Joseph said his brothers meant evil against him, but God meant it for...
choices: good, to preserve many lives
revenge, to prove Joseph was right
preserving life while excusing their guilt
steps.correctAnswer: good, to preserve many lives
steps.feedback: Correct. Joseph saw God's good purpose even through evil actions.
steps.hint: God's good purpose did not make the brothers' evil good.

steps.title: Providence
steps.prompt: Joseph's story teaches that God's providence means...
choices: God can work His good purposes even through hard events
evil choices stop being evil when God uses them
God's good purposes are always clear right away
steps.correctAnswer: God can work His good purposes even through hard events
steps.feedback: Right. God can work good purposes through hard events.
steps.hint: The Bible does not excuse evil, but it does show God's wise rule.

questions.prompt: Joseph's brothers sold him because of...
choices: jealousy and hatred
a plan to protect him
obedience to God's command
questions.reviewNote: Their action was sinful, even though God later used it.

questions.prompt: Joseph suffered unjustly, but God had not abandoned him.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: The Lord was with Joseph.

questions.prompt: Joseph said dream interpretation belonged to...
choices: God
Pharaoh
Joseph's family
questions.reviewNote: Joseph gave credit to God.

questions.prompt: God used Joseph in Egypt to...
choices: preserve many lives during famine
remove His promises from Jacob's family
make Joseph's brothers' sin good
questions.reviewNote: God used Joseph to preserve life.

questions.prompt: Joseph's words in Genesis 50:20 teach that...
choices: God can mean good even through evil human actions
evil choices become good if they have useful results
forgiveness removes the need to call sin evil
questions.correctAnswer: God can mean good even through evil human actions
questions.reviewNote: God's good purpose does not excuse evil actions.

lessons.title: Unit 2 Final Quiz
lessons.teacherSummary: Students complete a scored quiz on creation, image of God, the Fall, Cain and Abel, Noah, Babel, the patriarchs, and Joseph.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students complete a scored quiz on creation, image of God, the Fall, Cain and Abel, Noah, Babel, the patriarchs, and Joseph.

quiz.title: Creation to the Patriarchs: Unit 2 Final Quiz

questions.prompt: Genesis 1:1 teaches that...
choices: God created the heavens and the earth
God gave purpose to a world He did not create
God waited for creation to form itself
questions.correctAnswer: God created the heavens and the earth
questions.reviewNote: Genesis begins with God as Creator.

questions.prompt: Which answer best fits the Bible's creation week?
choices: God created in six days and rested on the seventh
God rested before creation was complete
God used long ages before giving the world meaning
questions.correctAnswer: God created in six days and rested on the seventh
questions.reviewNote: Scripture presents creation in six days with rest on the seventh.

questions.prompt: Human beings have dignity because...
choices: they are made by God in His image
they are able to choose their own value
they become valuable when they are useful
questions.correctAnswer: they are made by God in His image
questions.reviewNote: Human dignity comes from being made in God's image.

questions.prompt: Genesis teaches that God created humanity male and female in His image.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Male and female are both made in God's image.

questions.section: Part B: Fall and Spread of Sin
questions.prompt: The serpent tempted Eve by pushing her to...
choices: doubt God's word and goodness
remember God's command more clearly
ask Adam to worship with her
questions.reviewNote: The serpent twisted God's word and challenged His goodness.

questions.section: Part B: Fall and Spread of Sin
questions.prompt: Sin brought death and brokenness into God's good world.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Sin brought death and brokenness after creation.

questions.section: Part B: Fall and Spread of Sin
questions.prompt: Genesis 3:15 points forward to...
choices: God's promised victory over the serpent
people fixing sin by their own effort
sin slowly becoming less serious
questions.correctAnswer: God's promised victory over the serpent
questions.reviewNote: Genesis 3:15 gives the first promise of rescue.

questions.section: Part B: Fall and Spread of Sin
questions.prompt: God warned Cain that sin was...
choices: crouching at the door
mostly just a feeling Cain could handle later
mostly Abel's problem
questions.reviewNote: God warned Cain before Cain murdered Abel.

questions.prompt: The ark was God's appointed way to...
choices: rescue Noah, his family, and the animals
show Noah's skill apart from God's command
delay judgment without dealing with sin
questions.correctAnswer: rescue Noah, his family, and the animals
questions.reviewNote: God commanded the ark for rescue.

questions.prompt: The rainbow was a sign of God's covenant promise after the Flood.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: The rainbow was a covenant sign.

questions.prompt: The people at Babel wanted to...
choices: make a name for themselves
scatter in obedience to God
build an altar after rescue
questions.reviewNote: Babel centered on proud self-glory.

questions.prompt: God confused the languages at Babel so that...
choices: their proud plan could not continue as before
all people would worship in one city
they could finish the tower with more care
questions.correctAnswer: their proud plan could not continue as before
questions.reviewNote: God stopped their proud plan and scattered them.

questions.prompt: God's promises to Abraham included...
choices: land, offspring, and blessing
flood, rainbow, and languages
law, temple, and exile
questions.reviewNote: Land, offspring, and blessing are major promise themes.

questions.prompt: Isaac's birth showed that God keeps promises even when they seem impossible.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Isaac's birth showed God's faithfulness.

questions.prompt: Jacob's story shows God's plan continuing...
choices: despite family sin and conflict
because Jacob's family usually made faithful choices
because Jacob finally made up for earlier sins
questions.reviewNote: God kept His promises despite family failures.

questions.prompt: Joseph's brothers sold him because of...
choices: jealousy and hatred
a plan to protect him from famine
obedience to a command from God
questions.reviewNote: Their action was sinful, even though God later used it.

questions.prompt: Joseph's words in Genesis 50:20 teach that...
choices: God can mean good even through evil human actions
evil choices become good when they help later
forgiveness removes the need to call sin evil
questions.correctAnswer: God can mean good even through evil human actions
questions.reviewNote: God's good purpose does not excuse evil actions.

questions.prompt: Which order is correct?
choices: Creation, Fall, Flood, Babel, Abraham, Joseph
Creation, Babel, Joseph, Fall, Flood, Abraham
Flood, Creation, Fall, Abraham, Babel, Joseph
questions.correctAnswer: Creation, Fall, Flood, Babel, Abraham, Joseph
questions.reviewNote: This is the correct order for this unit's Bible timeline.
